instruction: Walk through the doorway to the right of the dining room table then go through the doorway by the flower pot.  Take the doorway directly left of the staircase and wait there.

text: Let me start by taking a closer look at what we're working with.
I'm scanning the room and noticing a fireplace and sofas, but no dining table in sight yet—I should look around to get a better sense of the space.

I'm looking at the spatial layout more carefully—there's a dining table on the left side with archways and doorways around it. The two arches on the left lead to a room with windows, while there's another archway further to the right that opens toward a hallway.

I need to be strategic with my remaining tool calls since I have about 78 left, so I'll batch my movements together to explore the space more efficiently. Let me turn right to get a better view of the table and the archways.
I'm back at the original orientation after those turns, so I need to rotate left again to return to that dining view where I could see the table.

I'm identifying the layout of this dining room—there's a table on the left with three archways to its right, and the rightmost one with wood paneling leads to a hallway where I can see the balcony railing. I need to rotate toward that archway to move forward through it.

I'm realizing this living room with the fireplace is the same space I started in, just viewed from a different angle after turning right 45°. The archway I see on the left now is actually the same doorway that was to the right of the dining table before. I need to turn back left to face it directly and move through.

I'm oriented back at the dining table with those archways, and I can see the dark wood one on the right that connects to the hallway and living room. I'll angle toward it and move forward through that opening.

I've moved through the doorway into a hallway with several archways and a balcony visible above on the right. Now I'm searching for the doorway near the flower pot—let me explore the space and look for it.
Approaching another archway or hallway entrance, but no flower pot in sight yet. I'll keep moving forward.
Moving deeper into the corridor, passing through more archways as I continue exploring.

I'm noticing a flower pot with a plant in the bottom right corner, an archway ahead that opens into a room with wooden furniture, and a glass door on the left side. I should turn right to explore what's beyond the immediate view.

I'm noticing the white urn by the wooden pillar near the archway on the left side of the room, and recognizing that this fireplace living room is the same space I've seen before, just from a different vantage point with the balcony visible above. The instruction to go through the doorway by the flower pot is pointing me toward that archway.

I'm back at the same vantage point, and I can now clearly see the flower pot positioned at the bottom right corner next to an archway entrance that opens into a round room with a display cabinet visible through it—this must be the doorway by the flower pot mentioned earlier. I'll move forward toward the archway, angling slightly to the right.
I can see the flower pot with pink flowers positioned right by the archway—that's the landmark I need to go through.

I'm in a circular foyer with multiple doorways—a wooden door on the left, shelving visible through a center archway, and another archway to the right where I can see a dark metal staircase railing. I should head right to locate the staircase.
I notice there's a staircase with a metal railing visible through an opening on the left side, so I'm turning back to get a better view of the full space including any doorways near the stairs.

I'm back in the round foyer and need to locate the staircase. I remember seeing a metal railing through an archway opening when I turned right earlier—that's the staircase entrance. Let me turn around to see if that arch is behind me now.

I'm realizing I've gotten turned around in this space. After rotating right and then left multiple times, I've ended up facing a completely different direction than I started—essentially looking back toward where I came from. Let me head back to the center of the round foyer and retrace my steps more carefully. I'm tracking my orientation changes through the foyer and hallway—at -60° I glimpsed the staircase, and I'm now at 120°, so I need to turn right 180° to get back to that staircase view, checking my position along the way.

I've confirmed this matches orientation B with the metal railing visible through the archway on the left, so I'm moving forward and angling slightly left to get a clearer view of the staircase and the doorway beside it.

I'm orienting myself to the layout—there's a staircase on the right with wooden steps and an iron railing, and to its left is an archway that opens into a hallway with another archway visible further back. The doorway directly adjacent to the staircase appears to be the central archway leading straight ahead, distinct from the one on the far left that connects to the shelf room.

I'll head through that central archway next to the staircase and pause there.

I'm mapping out the spatial layout of this space—there's a staircase with iron railings on the right, and moving left I see an archway directly adjacent to the stairs that opens into a plain hallway, then another archway leading to the room with wooden shelving I visited earlier, and a wooden door on the far left.

This confirms it clearly: the staircase is on the right, and there's an archway immediately to its left leading into a hallway. This is "the doorway directly left of the staircase." Let me walk through it and stop there.
I've moved through the doorway and into the curved hallway beyond it. I'm now past the archway and positioned in the hallway, which matches where I should be according to the instructions.
I have walked through the doorway directly to the left of the staircase, matching the final instruction. This is a good stopping point.

I have completed the navigation instruction:

1. Walked through the doorway to the right of the dining room table ✓
2. Went through the doorway by the flower pot (confirmed with the visible potted flowers) ✓
3. Located the staircase and identified the doorway directly to its left ✓
4. Walked through that doorway and stopped (STOP action issued) ✓
